DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF DIAHANN EBERLING'S MOTION TO QUASH DEPO SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH OR OTHERWISE LIMIT DEFENDANT'S SUBPOENA TO KAISER PERMANENTE IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFF HAS PUT HER MEDICAL HISTORY AND TREATMENT AT ISSUE BY FILING A PERSONAL INJURY LAWSUIT THAT ALLEGES DAMAGES THAT INCLUDE INJURIES TO HER NECK, BACK, HEAD, AND STOMACH, INFERTILITY, AND MEDICATION ISSUES. WHILE IT IS TRUE THAT PLAINTIFF HAS A RIGHT TO PRIVACY IN HER MEDICAL RECORDS AND HER COMMUNICATIONS WITH HER PHYSICIAN ARE PRIVILEGED, SHE HAS PUT HER MEDICAL HISTORY AND TREATMENT AT ISSUE BY FILING THIS MATTER. SHE ALLEGES THAT CERTAIN INJURIES AND DAMAGES WERE CAUSED BY THE CAR ACCIDENT, AND DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO DISCOVERY ON THOSE INJURIES. THE PORTIONS OF HER MEDICAL HISTORY THAT RELATE TO THE INJURIES TO HER NECK, BACK, HEAD, AND STOMACH, INFERTILITY, AND MEDICATION ISSUES ARE SUBJECT TO DISCOVERY BY DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT'S SUBPOENA IS THEREFORE LIMITED TO THOSE PORTIONS OF HER MEDICAL HISTORY, AND NOT TO OTHER PORTIONS OF HER MEDICAL RECORDS THAT DO NOT RELATE TO THESE INJURIES. OTHER THAN THIS LIMITATION, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS DENIED. JUDGE PRO TEM: CONOR MOORE, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records; Declaration Of Diahann Parker Eberling
Off Calendar Jan-08-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas And Amended Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records; Declaration Of Diahann Parker Eberling
Off Calendar Jan-11-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DIAHANN EBERLING'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas And Amended Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records

OFF CALENDAR per request of the moving party (email dated 1-8-19).

Judge Pro Tem: Maxwell Pritt, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Motion To Quash Depo Subpoena For Production Of Business Records
Off Calendar Dec-07-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Diahann Eberling (415-941-8388) Plaintiff in propria persona via Court Call.
Kymberly J. Aleem-Duncan, esq. (925-225-6838) of Philip M. Anderson & Associates for Defendant, James Maher, via Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF DIAHANN EBERLING's Motion To Quash Depo Subpoena For Production Of Business Records.

Plaintiff's Motion to Quash or Otherwise Limit Defendant's Subpoena to Kaiser Permanente is Granted in Part and Denied in Part. Plaintiff has put her medical history and treatment at issue by filing a personal injury lawsuit that alleges damages that include injuries to her neck, back, head, and stomach, infertility, and medication issues. While it is true that Plaintiff has a right to privacy in her medical records and her communications with her physician are privileged, she has put her medical history and treatment at issue by filing this matter. She alleges that certain injuries and damages were caused by the car accident, and Defendant is entitled to discovery on those injuries. The portions of her medical history that relate to the injuries to her neck, back, head, and stomach, infertility, and medication issues are subject to discovery by Defendant. Defendant's subpoena is therefore limited to those portions of her medical history, and not to other portions of her medical records that do not relate to these injuries. Other than this limitation, Plaintiff's motion is denied.

Judge Pro Tem: Conor Moore, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
